title: Liquidation reveals the trillion was never there
hook: A billionaire's trillion is not a pile of money. It is the market's belief about assets, sharp only because nobody is selling. Liquidate it and you sample at the worst possible rate; the number collapses on contact. You cannot redistribute a posterior.
hold_line: The value exists in the unsampled state. Forced sale is a wave hitting a price that only held while it was not being sold into. Selling destroys part of the thing being sold.
real_counter: Liquidation still yields something large. Concede residual; hold that the headline was never distributable.

title: Redistributing money is redistributing the fruit, not the orchard
hook: Wealth is a generator function: knowing which games exist and how the rules work. Hand most people $3,000 and it decays back to subsistence because the generator stayed elsewhere. Outputs are the wrong redistribution target.
hold_line: You can move the amplifier but not the signal that aims it. Money with no model is amplification with no steering.
real_counter: This can read as 'poor lack skill'. State first: wealth ≈ generator × network position; position is inherited/luck/access, not merit. Cash moves neither.

title: The most fundable ideas are the worst risk-adjusted bets
hook: Fundability measures how crowded a thesis already is. Abundant capital chases the same fundable idea; everyone launches the same wave at once, and the waves interfere. You get priced at the peak and paid on the residue.
hold_line: Each founder is privately rational; the aggregate competes away the opportunity. Interference is invisible at funding time because it settles after the check clears.
real_counter: Some crowded waves build shared infrastructure. Correct: distinguish constructive from destructive interference; claim targets waves competing for the same rent on a substrate that pays once.

title: It is now easier to make real value locally than to change the world
hook: The scarce resource is stationarity: a piece of the world that holds still long enough to build durable value. Wide world-changing plays destroy their own stationarity through interference. Narrow local plays are the substrates still standing still.
hold_line: Fast-and-wide starts broad but cannot narrow or steer; cheap-and-slow stays focused and captures what it builds.
real_counter: Some things require global scale. Yes: claim is about where capturable value concentrates for most builders, not that scale never wins.

title: No single AI lab will hold the frontier again
hook: The frontier is reconstructible from cheaper parts: ensemble weaker models, beat the frontier, recurse. A position rebuildable from commodity parts earns no durable rent. Labs are spending like they are building a moat and buying a packing lead coverage erases.
hold_line: Capability-per-cost is the dimension the market learns to reconstruct. Cost competition races to zero by construction.
real_counter: Ensembling wins on benchmarks, not long-horizon agentic work. Concede domain limit: holds wherever tasks are cheaply checkable.

title: The benchmark-able frontier is the one labs cannot defend
hook: When you can cheaply check if an AI did well, merit routes around brand. Incumbents are incentivized to move evaluation from external to internal: trust our taste. The taste-and-trust frontier is the defensible one. Watch eval move.
hold_line: Whoever controls whether a capability is cheaply checkable controls whether merit or brand wins it.
real_counter: Taste genuinely matters. True; the uncheckable parts are where position legitimately dominates, which is why incumbents prefer to compete there.

title: Cheap trustworthy validation is the whole game
hook: When validation is expensive, it gets skipped; skipped validation means position decides instead of merit. The master variable is the cost of a check you can trust. Cheap trustworthy audits keep fields merit-routable instead of collapsing into who you know.
hold_line: An audit pays validation cost once through a trusted party, so everyone downstream gets the external check cheaply.
real_counter: Audits can be captured/Goodharted. True; trustworthy is load-bearing. Keeping auditor honest is the next problem, not a refutation.

title: Information economics has no theory of the target moving while you learn it
hook: Search, signaling, prices: info-econ assumes the thing you learn holds still long enough to learn it. It is a theory of reaching information equilibrium. It lacks native account of what happens when the world changes faster than markets validate change.
hold_line: Classical results are the slow-change limit, not wrong. This is the fast-change case.
real_counter: Rational expectations handles anticipated change. Reply: asymmetry is temporal, not rationality failure; slow signal is unavailable at decision time.

title: There is a phase transition where trust can no longer form
hook: Let λ be world-change rate and μ market-validation rate. Below λ≈μ you get settled trust. Above it, observations go stale before enough accumulate: permanent disequilibrium. AI pushes λ up while μ stays human-paced.
hold_line: Validation is human-bounded; generation is not. λ/μ rises mechanically.
real_counter: μ also rises with tools. Partly, but terminal validator is human judgment/trust, not infinitely accelerable.

title: Velocity itself biases you toward good news
hook: In a fast market, positive signal arrives fast and negative signal arrives slow. A firm deciding on the fast clock structurally under-samples slow bad news. The faster you run, the more your feedback diet skews toward confirmation.
hold_line: Speed does not hide bad news; it outruns it.
real_counter: Smart firms forecast the slow signal. Some do, but forecasts of slow harm are exactly what fast positive feedback crowds out.

title: Money has no internal motivation model, so it runs in circles
hook: Capital is capacity to act with no sense of what is worth doing: amplification with no steering. In a fast market it borrows direction from nearest signal, which is other capital. Money steered by money, with reality cut out, is a runaway.
hold_line: A system responding only to itself accelerates because the only signal left is its own. Human judgment is steering, not fuel.
real_counter: Returns are real-world signal. Returns are fast signals that can decouple from slow reality; the decoupling window is where runaways live.

title: A floor is worth more the more you build on it, and it can never be cheapest
hook: Compete on cost and you race to zero because cost is reconstructible. Be a dependable floor and you appreciate because others build on you, which requires trust that you will not move. You need reliability, not cheapest or best.
hold_line: A stable floor lets everyone above stop re-checking their foundation and spend energy building.
real_counter: This is network effects/lock-in. Distinct: asset is dependability, not network size. A trusted small floor beats a large erratic one.

title: Defense is about to beat offense, because recognition got cheap and generation did not
hook: Disruption worked because offense was faster than defense: inflict change before anyone learned resistance. AI flips this: novel disruption is open-ended generation; recognizing and adapting is pattern matching. If defense outruns offense, the world can push back in time.
hold_line: Record state, let AI find drift, keep human authority to say no: observe, model, reject.
real_counter: AI accelerates offense too. Load-bearing falsifier: if genuinely-new-state generation accelerates as much as recognition, reversal fails.
